title: seriphidium caerulescens subsp. gallicum
intro: Seriphidium caerulescens subsp. gallicum, also known as French blue sage, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function, and has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its antispasmodic and diuretic properties. In herbal preparations, Seriphidium caerulescens subsp. gallicum can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in aromatherapy and topically in ointments and salves.
